name: Is stuff I tell Google AI Overview protected like lawyer talks? I'm scared after reading about that NY case.
description: No, AI inputs waive privilege per NY Heppner ruling—treat AI as third party, no protection.[1][4]

mainEntity.name: Is stuff I tell Google AI Overview protected like lawyer talks? I'm scared after reading about that NY case.

acceptedAnswer.text: No, AI inputs waive privilege per NY Heppner ruling—treat AI as third party, no protection.[1][4]
acceptedAnswer.description: No, inputs to consumer AI tools like Google AI Overviews are not protected by attorney-client privilege, as ruled in the Southern District of New York Heppner case where AI was deemed a third party with no confidentiality expectation.[1][3][4] The court held that AI platforms' privacy policies allow data use for training and disclosure, waiving privilege even if inputs were originally privileged. Factors: AI disclaims legal advice, no attorney relationship exists, and independent use doesn't qualify as work product. Avoid inputting sensitive legal info into public AI; use lawyer-directed secure tools instead. Save all records if litigation involves AI use.
